title: The Exact Value of R(B22, B22)
lean_gate_reason: The exact statement is already covered by prior art via the Rousseau-Sheehan prime-power theorem cited in the canonical source, so Lean would formalize a known result rather than shorten the path to a new publication packet.
micro_paper_assessment: Fails the micro-paper lane on novelty. The local Paley(89) argument rederives an exact value already implied by the Rousseau-Sheehan prime-power theorem cited in the canonical source.
family_affinity: strong; diagonal book Ramsey endpoint closed by a Paley / strongly-regular lower-bound witness matched to a sourced one-step upper bound
generalization_signal: moderate; the witness template scales to other diagonal book cases aligned with self-complementary strongly regular graphs, but exact closure still needs a matching upper bound
strongest_honest_claim: The Paley(89) construction gives a clean rederivation of the known exact value R(B22, B22) = 90, already covered by the Rousseau-Sheehan prime-power theorem as cited in Wesley.
candidate_theorem_slice: R(B22, B22) = 90 is a known prime-power diagonal book Ramsey value; the local witness reproduces the classical lower-bound mechanism rather than creating a new frontier theorem.
